title: Add 'claude' alias to config install CLIENT_ALIASES registry
description: The CLIENT_ALIASES map in config.ts is missing a 'claude' key, causing 'fabric config install --clients=claude' to throw 'unknown client'. Add 'claude' as an alias mapping to the existing ClientKind 'ClaudeCodeCLI'. Add matching i18n labels to both locale files and extend the config-install test to verify the new alias works.

files.change: Add 'claude': 'ClaudeCodeCLI' entry to the CLIENT_ALIASES Record<string, ClientKind> at L13-L31 — this is a one-line addition that resolves the 'unknown client' error.
Add 'cli.config.clients.claude' label key following the convention of adjacent client label keys so the install summary prints a human-readable client name.
Mirror the new 'cli.config.clients.claude' key added to en.ts with Chinese translation.
Add test case asserting that parseClientFilter(['claude']) returns a ClientKind of ClaudeCodeCLI and does not throw; verify --dry-run produces a valid server entry.

implementation: Open packages/cli/src/commands/config.ts and locate the CLIENT_ALIASES object (L13-L31); add the line '"claude": "ClaudeCodeCLI"' following the pattern of adjacent entries.

Check if existing tests cover CLIENT_ALIASES resolution; if packages/cli/__tests__/config-install.test.ts exists, add a describe block for the 'claude' alias; otherwise create the file following the Vitest pattern from __tests__/init-nondestructive.test.ts.
Open packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts and add 'cli.config.clients.claude': 'Claude Code CLI' in the config.clients section.
Open packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts and add the matching zh-CN key.
Run 'pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test' to confirm no regressions on cursor/codex/gemini installs.

reference.pattern: CLIENT_ALIASES is a flat Record<string, ClientKind> map; new aliases require no new ClientKind type and no new writer class
reference.examples: exploration-architecture.json (CLIENT_ALIASES, L13-L31): 'claude key is absent — add claude: ClaudeCodeCLI'; writer.ts (ClientKind, L1-L8): 'ClaudeCodeCLI already in union — no new type needed'

criteria: 'fabric config install --clients=claude --dry-run' exits 0 and prints a server entry for ClaudeCodeCLI
Emitted config schema matches {mcpServers: {fabric: {command: 'node', args: [serverPath]}}} format used by ClaudeCodeCLIWriter
No regression: 'fabric config install --clients=cursor --dry-run' and '--clients=codex --dry-run' both still exit 0
New test case in config-install.test.ts passes
